name: Omelet & Noodle Dinner
recipeInstructions:
This omelet-noodle combination is a bit of nostalgia for people who loved the Chinese-American restaurants of the '50s and '60s.
1. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok or skillet over high heat. When the pan is nearly smoking, add the bean sprouts, celery, and snow peas. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute and immediately pour the vegetables into a work bowl.
2. Add scallions, diced ham, and soy sauce to the bowl with the vegetables. Sprinkle in cornstarch and mix well. Add eggs and water, and mix.
3. Set the mixture aside. Cook the noodles for 5 minutes in boiling salted water. Drain and set aside.
4. In a soup pot over low heat, combine sesame oil, minced garlic, and ginger. Cook for 1 minute.
5. Add red pepper flakes, chicken broth, clam juice, soy sauce, vinegar, and sherry to the pot. Cover, increase heat to medium, and boil for 5 minutes.
6. Heat oil (about 3/8-inch depth) in a large skillet over high heat. Add 3-ounce ladles of the egg mixture into the skillet. Cook until edges puff, about 1 minute, then flip and cook on the other side for about 30 seconds. Remove omelets and drain on towels.
7. When it's time for dinner, divide noodles and broth between 4 soup bowls. Make a bed of shredded lettuce. Arrange omelets on top of lettuce. Toss some cilantro on top and serve.
